reason: The item cleanly targets boundary-aware evaluation of a comparative hazard claim under two independent variables (SOC and heating power), forcing the solver to separate what is supported from what is overgeneralized and to name the omitted factor. Diagnostic value is strong because it penalizes “directionally correct therefore always true” reasoning, but the short-answer format can admit partially correct responses and grading may be sensitive to how explicitly the solver articulates the power-dependence qualifier.
